payload.summary: Planned a coordinated OpenSpec change to make autopilot and its lifecycle skills vendor-neutral across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini/Jules. Gate 1 selected the provider-neutral dispatch adapter approach with provider-specific model mapping and a manual CLI smoke path.
completed_work: Created proposal.md with three approaches and recorded Gate 1 selection of Approach 2.
Created strict delta specs for skill-workflow, agent-archetypes, coordination-bridge, and configuration.
Created runtime contracts, tasks.md, and coordinated work-packages.yaml.
Validated OpenSpec strict mode, work-package schema/DAG/lock keys, and parallel scope checks.

decisions.title: Use provider-neutral dispatch adapters
decisions.rationale: The operator selected Approach 2 so phase dispatch becomes a runtime contract shared by Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini/Jules instead of a Claude-only Agent surface.

decisions.title: Resolve archetypes to provider-specific model IDs
decisions.rationale: Logical archetypes remain stable, but concrete model names must be selected per provider to avoid passing Claude aliases to Codex or Gemini.

decisions.title: Require manual provider smoke coverage
decisions.rationale: The success bar includes an end-to-end smoke path that can be manually triggered from a chosen CLI provider, so static validation alone is insufficient.

next_steps: Start implementation with contract and model-resolution tests before adapter code.

relevant_files.description: Provider dispatch payload and result contract
